# .github/lint/search-v3-forbidden-imports.txt
#
# Consumed by `make lint-search-v3` (see Makefile). One symbol per line;
# `#` comments and blank lines are ignored. Every hit under src/ that is not
# on the whitelist below fails the lint.
#
# Rule (RFC-107 §S + PRD-045 FR12; #1205 SIGSEGV):
#   The LanceDB native hybrid combine (`_combine_hybrid_results` /
#   `_normalize_scores` via `pyarrow.compute`) segfaulted the api under
#   stack-test. Fix `0fe0854b` bypassed the native combine via the
#   Python-side `search_bm25 + search_vector + rrf_fuse` fan-out in
#   `retrieval.py`. No code path may re-enable the native combine until
#   #1205's root cause is fixed upstream (LanceDB / pyarrow) and
#   independently verified.

# --- FORBIDDEN symbols ---
# Each line is a literal fixed-string. Presence in any src/ file (outside
# the whitelist) fails the lint.
_combine_hybrid_results
_normalize_scores

# --- WHITELIST (paths, one per line, `WHITELIST:` prefix) ---
# Files listed here may reference the forbidden symbols. Currently empty —
# no source file has legitimate need. Add sparingly, and only with an ADR
# update explaining why the guardrail is being relaxed for that file.
#
# Format:
#   WHITELIST: relative/path/to/file.py
#
# (no entries)
